title: Sweet Filipino Bilo-Bilo Ginataang Bilo-Bilo
description: A quick-fix Filipino dessert featuring chewy bilo-bilo and a sweet ginata sauce. This dish is perfect for a comforting and sweet treat.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the crushed gingersnap cookies and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the water, granulated sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Add the coconut milk and plantain fritter dough to the saucepan. Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the dough has broken down and the sauce has thickened further.
Add the anise extract and green food coloring, if using. Cook for an additional minute.
Add the bilo-bilo to the saucepan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they are cooked through and have absorbed some of the sauce.
Serve the ginata bilo-bilo warm, garnished with additional gingersnap cookies and a drizzle of the ginata sauce, if desired.
